fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong revenue expansion but high valuation and thin margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4% — significant earnings surprise.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current quarter (286.1%) and fiscal year (179.8%).
Gross margin 30.1% indicates good pricing power on its core product.
Strong liquidity with current ratio 5.03 and quick ratio 3.98.
negatives: Forward P/E of 66.03 and Price/Sales of 33.15 indicate a very high valuation.
Profit margin is thin at 0.2%, suggesting profitability challenges despite revenue growth.
Return on equity at 1.3% is low, indicating inefficient use of shareholder capital.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 is very high, indicating significant leverage.
Beta of 3.83 suggests high volatility relative to the market.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) has seen a surge in its share price, with recent articles highlighting its potential as a future stock to buy, despite some mixed sentiment in the broader fuel cell sector. While the company recently beat Q1 earnings estimates by a significant margin, its stock has also experienced some down days. The company's strong revenue growth and positive analyst sentiment are key factors driving interest, though its high valuation metrics warrant consideration.
